revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[bulleted list]] directly using [[HTML]]'s [[HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23127601988|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Bulleted_list%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:List}} {{Template shortcuts|clear=right|blist|bulleted|ulist|unordered list}} This template may be used to generate a [[:en:Bulleted list|Bulleted list]] directly using [[:en:HTML|HTML]]'s [[:en:HTML element|{{tag|ul}} and {{tag|li}} list elements]]. A typical application is within captions, where the equivalent [[Help:Wikitext|wiki markup]] (asterisks at the starts of new lines) may be awkward or impossible to use. == Usage == {{bulleted list |item1 |item2 |...}} == Parameters == * Positional parameters (1, 2, 3...) – these are the list items. If no list items are present, the module will output nothing. * class – a custom class for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. plainlinks. * style – a custom css style for the {{tag|div}} tags surrounding the list, e.g. font-size: 90%;. * list_style – a custom css style for the list itself. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item_style – a custom css style for all of the list items (the {{tag|li}} tags). The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. * item1_style, item2_style, item3_style... – custom css styles for each of the list items. The format is the same as for the {{para|style}} parameter. == Examples == [[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]] 
[[File:Example.png|thumb|100px|{{bulleted list |one |two |three}}]]
{{clear}} == See also == {{HTML lists}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
